spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Mobile-first, compact but readable, dense lists. Control Room Grid style.

surface_strategies.cards: Flat background (surface), 1px subtle border, no shadow. Active/focus state gets a tinted neon border.
surface_strategies.terminal_window: Retro-Futurism: Deep black background, emerald text, monospace font, subtle 1px border. No border radius (sharp edges).
surface_strategies.header: Solid surface background with a sharp 1px bottom border.

components.navigation: Bottom tab bar for mobile view (Scan, History, Settings) fixed at the bottom. Fixed header with app logo and active status.
components.buttons: Square or very slightly rounded. Primary buttons have solid primary color, secondary buttons have border-only (outline).
components.inputs: High contrast. Deep black background, glowing primary border on focus, large tappable area (min-h-[44px]) for mobile.
components.badges: Solid background with 20% opacity of the severity color, 1px solid border of the severity color, and solid text color.
components.loader: ASCII style progress (e.g., '[=====>   ]') or pulsing block. No standard circular spinners.
components.tables_lists: Use dense lists/cards for mobile, rather than wide data tables. Stack host, port, state vertically if needed.

hero_background.description: Dark, glowing neon scanlines abstract background, useful for empty state or header hero graphic. Use with an opacity overlay to keep text readable.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the application as a mobile-first responsive web app, optimized for small screens with a bottom navigation bar layout.
Install and use JetBrains Mono and IBM Plex Sans from Google Fonts.
Use Phosphor Icons (or lucide-react) for crisp, technical icons.
Implement a 'Terminal Mode' box for the live Nmap output while scanning, with auto-scroll and JetBrains Mono.
Parse the results into structured cards with Nessus-style severity badges.
Ensure all interactive elements have a 'data-testid' attribute (e.g., 'data-testid="target-input"').
Avoid raw HTML <select> or <input type='date'>; use Shadcn UI components and customize them with sharp edges and dark themes.
